AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Functionality - app functioning, ease of use, navigation, flow logic
ParentText: Investigate a popular app targeted at Australia's youth that promotes health and wellbeing (for examples, see the Youth Focus website). Write a review on the app and share results. The review should include:

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Aesthetics - graphic design, overall visual appeal, colour scheme and stylistic consistency
ParentText: Investigate a popular app targeted at Australia's youth that promotes health and wellbeing (for examples, see the Youth Focus website). Write a review on the app and share results. The review should include:

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Information - contains high quality information from a credible source
ParentText: Investigate a popular app targeted at Australia's youth that promotes health and wellbeing (for examples, see the Youth Focus website). Write a review on the app and share results. The review should include:

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Subjective quality - would you recommend this app? (Source: Queensland University of Technology, Mobile Application Rating scale (MARS))
ParentText: Investigate a popular app targeted at Australia's youth that promotes health and wellbeing (for examples, see the Youth Focus website). Write a review on the app and share results. The review should include:

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: In small groups, devise and share scenarios and/or case studies that illustrate how young people might find themselves relying on digital media such as websites and online health practitioners to access the health information and support they need.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: View My Health Record identify what the My Health Record is, the benefits of having a record and the possible privacy and security issues.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Brainstorm for a list of consumer rights when accessing healthcare. Record your ideas on a Padlet. Watch the video outlining the Australian Charter of Healthcare Rights. How did your list compare to that of the Australian Charter of Healthcare Rights?

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Create an infographic that lists the rights and responsibilities of consumers accessing health services.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Develop a brochure, pamphlet or visual presentation that could be displayed in a hospital or doctors' rooms, informing patients of their rights and avenues for complaint about a particular health system. Use the Australian Charter of Healthcare Rights to access information about healthcare rights.to access information about healthcare rights.

AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: In small groups, discuss:

ExampleText: Why it is important to have access to quality health information?

ExampleText: How do you know if the information you are accessing online is reliable health information?

AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Refer to the Australian Commission on Safety and Quality in Healthcare poster Top tips for finding good health information online or the video and compare this with the points raised in your discussion.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Students explore and list the health services for youth that could be accessed in their local area or online. This could involve a literal walk in the local community or a search of the internet. After creating the list of services, each student should be allocated one or two services to review. The review should include:

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: The service name and contact information

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: The type of service provided, including a classification of the services according to the dimensions of health and wellbeing that they may promote and an explanation of how the service promotes the dimensions identified

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: The cost of the service or any other requirements, such as a Medicare card

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Any factors that may impact on the ability of youth to access the service

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: A critique of the health service, such as reliability and credibility.

Subject: Health and Human Development
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Youth health literacy
ExampleText: Student reviews could then be collated into a directory of health services and published on the class website or blog to enable all students to access the information.

ExampleText: Unit 3 Australia's health in a globalised world

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
ExampleText: Area of Study 1: Understanding health and wellbeing

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Outcome 1:

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain the complex, dynamic and global nature of health and wellbeing, interpret and apply Australia's health status data, and analyse variations in health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Examples of learning activities

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Create or collect a visual image of a 'healthy' person. Annotate all of the characteristics that you think make a person healthy. Share this with classmates and identify common themes in the pictures. Determine if there is a focus on physical health or on all dimensions. Are any dimensions not included? Why/why not?

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Brainstorm examples of the physical, social, emotional, mental and spiritual dimensions of health and create a visual display to demonstrate understanding of each dimension of health and wellbeing.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Create or find everyday case studies of people to identify different dimensions of health and wellbeing; for example, well-known stories from the media or fictional storylines and characters. Consider how the different dimensions of health and wellbeing may impact on other aspects of people's lives. Create a mind map that shows how the dimensions can impact on each other, starting with the scenario in the case studies.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Collect or create images to create a visual story that shows how health and wellbeing is dynamic.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Conduct a survey of a range of people asking what health and wellbeing means to them. Use the findings to explain how health and wellbeing is a subjective concept.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Using a list of statements that represent physical, social, emotional, mental and spiritual dimensions of health and wellbeing, in pairs, classify them into the corresponding dimension of health. Use the statements to construct a mind map of health dimensions using mind mapping software such as SmartDraw, Mind42, MindMeister, Mindomo or Bubbl.us to develop a concept map.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Consider how one dimension of health and wellbeing can impact on another dimension. Write a travelling case study. In pairs, begin a case study and outline the main characters' physical health and wellbeing. Pass your story to the next pair who continue the case by describing how the physical health and wellbeing can influence mental health and wellbeing. The case then gets passed to the next pair who add another dimension, and so on. This continues until all dimensions are covered.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Use a lotus diagram or other graphic organiser to summarise the concepts of health and wellbeing, including different dimensions (and relationships between them).

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Brainstorm factors that could contribute to different experiences of illness experienced by two people with the same disease.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Draw an outline of a body and inside the outline record a range of characteristics that would show this person to have optimal health and wellbeing across all dimensions. In small groups, determine what this person can do, or be, as a result of these characteristics, and record them outside the body.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Draw an outline of Australia. Inside the outline record examples of optimal health and wellbeing for the whole population, thinking about all the dimensions ('All Australians would have...'). In small groups, determine what the Australian Government or population could do as a result of the examples of health and wellbeing recorded inside the map and list these outside the outline.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Draw an outline of the globe. Inside the outline record examples of optimal health and wellbeing for the whole world, thinking about all the dimensions ('All People would have...'). In small groups, determine what this would mean for the whole planet and record these ideas.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Explain the health status of Australians compared with that of other developed countries by comparing data such as life expectancy, healthy adjusted life expectancy and under-five mortality rate (available on the WHO website).Using these health status indicators or researching other health status indicators for other developed countries, draw conclusions about the health status of Australia compared to selected countries and present findings as a mind map.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Using measurements of health status, including burden of disease, health adjusted life expectancy, disability adjusted life years (DALYs), life expectancy, under-five mortality rate, mortality, morbidity, incidence and prevalence, HALE, YLD, YLL and self-assessed health status, create a poster or Quizlet Flashcards that shows an example of each indicator as well as explaining what each indicator is.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Create a headpiece for each health status indicator and play a game of celebrity heads where a student wears an indicator and asks clarifying questions to determine the indicator on their head. Questions can only be answered with 'yes' or 'no'.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Explore Gapminder and use its online tools to analyse and interpret data and graphs in relation to changes in mortality and morbidity over time. Gapminder's motto is 'Unveiling the beauty of statistics for a fact-based world view'.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Access the most recent version of Australia's Health which is published by the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare every two years. Create a mind map identifying aspects of Australia's health where we have made positive advancements, as well as aspects that we need to improve.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Identify the strengths and weaknesses of self-assessed health status as a measure of the health status of a population. Debate whether it is a valid measure of health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Undertake a 'speed dating' type activity where each student is given a specific measure of health status and has to teach the concept to peers.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Tabulate factors that impact on health, categorising them as biological, sociocultural or environmental. For each one, write a brief explanation of the factor and describe how it can impact on health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Select a population group based on SES, gender, remoteness, age or Indigenous status. Using information available on the AIHW or the ABS, collect data about variations in health status. Complete a visual presentation that highlights a range of factors that can influence the variations in health status identified.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Use case studies, media releases or newspaper articles or film clips as a basis for identifying variations in health status among different population groups. in small groups, choose one population group and use websites such as the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare to investigate the health status of the chosen population group and reasons for the variations. Present findings as a mind map or a collaborative summary table that shows the variations in health status of the population group and the environmental, and sociocultural factors that contribute to these variations.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Access information about a men's health program such as the Men's Shed Association or the Western Bulldogs' Sons of the West. Identify areas of men's health that these programs target. Identify why the government is investing resources into men's health. Explain sociocultural and environmental factors that contribute to differences between male and female health.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Research the impact of smoking and vaping on health status by accessing the data and information available from Quit smoking, Quit vaping, Better health and Smoking and vaping. Summarise the key impacts in an info graphic.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Report on the key impacts on Australia's health status at a population level of overweight and obesity by accessing the prevalence and impacts data in the AIHW Overweight and Obesity overview

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Access the AIHW report 'Alcohol review' and identify some general trends in alcohol consumption over time and for different age groups. In groups, identify the harm of alcohol abuse to individual health status as well as to communities, workplaces and governments.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Using VicHealth's 'State of Salt' infographic as an example, create a similar infographic or poster for either fat or dairy intake. Include data on the impacts of the nutritional imbalance on health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: In small groups, create a poster or video advertisement encouraging a reduction in sugary drinks and showing how reducing sugar will benefit the health status of Australians. Access Rethink sugary drink for information.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Access media articles on a proposed 'sugar tax' as stimulus material, for example Australian sugary drink tax could prevent thousands of heart attacks and strokes. Then debate the topic: 'The Australian government should tax soft drink to save the health of Australians?'

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Create a mind map about fat, ensuring you include the different types of dietary fat (for example, saturated, trans, polyunsaturated, monounsaturated) and include the function of each type of fat in the body and its impact Australia's health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Research the nutrients in fruit and vegetables. Enter the nutrients into a table and explain the role of each nutrient in the body as well as the impact on health conditions and relevant health status indicators that can result from underconsumption of each nutrient.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Create a summary table of the following nutrients: fat, salt, sugar, calcium and fibre; indicate how they act as risk or protective nutrients to health status in Australia. Use the information to create an information brochure.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Biological, sociocultural and environmental factors that contribute to variations in health status between population groups

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: In groups, students select a population group based on SES (for example, high and low SES), gender, remoteness, age, Indigenous status, or country of birth. Each groups conduct research on the differences in health status for their chosen population group, using reliable data sources and risk factor information from the Australian Bureau of Statistics and AIHW report Australia's Health. They analyse this health data and combine it with their knowledge of biological, sociocultural and environmental factors to further analyse the possible reasons for the differences in health status within the chosen population group. Students create a visual presentation using iMovie, Prezi or PowerPoint that highlights a range of factors that can influence the variations in health status that have been identified. They share the presentations with classmates.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Students consider the following in their research:

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Outline the population group chosen

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Collect data using health status indicators and evaluate differences in health status within the chosen population group

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Choose relevant examples of biological, sociocultural and environmental factors and analyse how they can cause the differences in health status from above.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Understanding health and wellbeing
ExampleText: Area of Study 2: Promoting health in Australia

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Outcome 2:

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain changes to public health approaches, analyse improvements in population health over time and evaluate health promotion strategies and initiatives.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Examples of learning activities

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Access historical data on health status in Australia at AIHW Historical records of mortality. Analyse this data and draw conclusions about the overall changes in health status since 1900. Brainstorm possible reasons for improvements in health status.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Source a range of images of day-to-day life in Australia in the early 1900s (looking at aspects of life such as housing, workplaces, street life, transport, diets, etc.) from the State Library. Brainstorm possible causes of death and illness during these times, and any programs that might have been put in place to improve the rates of death and disease.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Develop a paragraph that describes the key concepts of the social model (not the principles). Use Google Images or Flickr to find images that represent the key ideas and create an annotated poster on the social model of health. Do the same for the biomedical model of health. Display the posters side by side and create a table that examines the strengths and limitations of each model in promoting improvements in health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Using the images of early life in Australia, annotate the various old public health initiatives that may have been implemented in relation to each image.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Create a timeline from 1900 to today to show when various models of health have been implemented to improve health outcomes for Australians. Use The National Museum of Australia Defining Moments timeline to research significant health initiatives from 1900. Add these to your timeline. Classify the examples as 'old public health', 'social model of health' or 'biomedical' and explain how they can lead to improvements in health.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: In small groups, students research a significant health issue in Australia such as smoking and vaping, skin cancer, road traffic accidents or obesity and overweight, looking at current data. Students research and select a health promotion initiative in response to their chosen issue and analyse the ways in which the initiative reflects action areas of the Ottawa Charter. Students analyse how the implementation of these actions can improve health outcomes for Australians. Students share their research with the class.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: In small groups, select one action area of the Ottawa Charter for health promotion, and brainstorm examples in response to a single health issue such as tobacco use, alcohol misuse, overweight and obesity, child safety or road safety. Use example to develop a poster display similar to SunSmart.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Google 'Public Health Campaign images in Australia' and choose a campaign to investigate. Consider: When did the campaign run? Who funded the campaign? Who was the campaign targeted at? What health issue was the campaign aiming to address? Identify the action areas of the Ottawa Charter reflected in the chosen campaign.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Access the National Tobacco Campaign website and create a timeline of tobacco control initiatives. Choose one initiative, identify action areas of the Ottawa Charter reflected in the initiative, and describe how it could improve health.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Visit How safe is your car? and identify action areas of the Ottawa Charter reflected in this initiative. Identify safety features present in new cars and explain how the technology aims to keep people safe. Research safety technology advances in cars over the last 60 or so years and present findings in a timeline. Compare the progression in safety advances to road death statistics and highlight any trends.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Visit SunSmart and create an infographic timeline of sun safety initiatives over the years. For each initiative, identify action areas of the Ottawa Charter reflected in the initiative and explain how they can improve health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Visit the Deadly Choices program and/or the Australian Indigenous Health Infonet. Choose one program, identify key aims of the program and analyse how it reflects the action areas of the Ottawa Charter for Health Promotion. Discuss how participation in the program might improve health for Indigenous Australians and promote social justice.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Visit the website of the Victorian Aboriginal Health Service and research the range of services available to promote health outcomes for Indigenous Australians. Analyse how your chosen program can promote social justice and contribute to improved health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: In pairs, access the Australian Dietary Guidelines and select one dietary guideline to research, including its terminology, current uptakes in Australia and strategies in the community that are provided to help implement it. Evaluate how this guideline might promote healthy eating.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Complete a table to compare the similarities and differences between the Australian Dietary Guidelines, the Australian Guide to Healthy Eating and the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Guide to Healthy Eating. Use this information to evaluate the ability of these tools to promote healthy eating.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Brainstorm all the reasons why nutritional change might be difficult to achieve. Classify these as: sociocultural, environmental and commercial factors.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Investigate the influence of sales and marketing techniques in nutritional choices using Food for health alliance. Draw conclusions about the influence of food companies on making nutritional improvements difficult to achieve.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Discuss the statement: 'Our environment is stacked against maintaining a healthy diet'. Brainstorm a list of factors contributing to the difficulty in achieving dietary change in Australia. Determine if these factors are environmental, sociocultural or commercial.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Use a lotus diagram or other graphic organiser to summarise the Australian healthcare system, including Medicare, Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme, NDIS or Private Health Insurance.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: In small groups, collect images of a range of healthcare services in the local area. Determine the range of services provided and how accessible they are to the community.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Access the most recent version of Australia's Health which is published by the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare every two years. Investigate how much Australia spends on health care each year and the types of services that the majority of funding is spent on. Discuss whether these services are a biomedical or social model approach.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Research one of: Medicare, Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme, NDIS or Private Health Insurance. Analyse how your aspect of the healthcare system can improve health status by considering access, funding, sustainability and equity. Present your findings to the class.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Create a Double Bubble (a type of Venn diagram: use a Google 'Images' search for examples) to compare Medicare and private health insurance and identify similarities and differences. Use this information to analyse the role of Medicare and private health insurance in improving health outcomes in relation to equity.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Research incentives for individuals to take out private health insurance. Discuss reasons why the Australian Government is encouraging people to have private health insurance, and how this might promote health outcomes.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Access the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and find the publication (click on 'Participants') My NDIS Pathway. Using this information and/or further web research, create a mind map explaining how the NDIS promotes the health of individuals with a disability. Consider its funding, ability to be sustained over time, accessibility and equity.

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: Health promotion analysis

Subject: Health and Human Development
UnitDescription: Australia's health in a globalised world
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - Promoting health in Australia
ExampleText: In small groups, students research a significant health issue in Australia such as smoking and vaping, skin cancer, road traffic accidents or obesity and overweight, looking at current data. Students research and select a health promotion initiative in response to their chosen issue and analyse the ways in which the initiative reflects action areas of the Ottawa Charter. Students analyse how the implementation of these actions can improve health outcomes for Australians. Students share their research with the class.

Create a brochure of frequently asked questions with corresponding answers designed to inform others about the use, relevance and limitations of the 'Human Development Index' as a measure.

Discuss the evident trends.

Identify how these factors may contribute to inequalities in health status. Share results and findings with other groups. Present findings using Comic Life, Animoto or Padlet

AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - Global health and human development
ExampleText: View the World Vision videos 'What is poverty' (2015, 3.04 min) and 'What causes poverty' (2015, 3.39 min) to explore similarities and differences in health status and human development between Australia and Timor Leste. Analyse how poverty contributes to these differences.
